structData.title: PMP deal troubleshooting — zero bid requests from SSP
structData.content:
A PMP (Private Marketplace) deal showing zero bid requests means the SSP is not sending any auction opportunities to your DSP for this deal. This is different from losing bids — you are not even receiving the bid requests in the first place.

HOW TO DIAGNOSE
Call getDealBidStream with hours=24. If bidRequestsReceived=0, the problem is upstream of bidding.
Call getDealStatus to check activation on both sides.
Call getSeatMapping to check the most common cause: seat ID mismatch.

CAUSE 1 — SEAT ID MISMATCH (most common, ~60% of cases)
The getSeatMapping tool returns two fields:
  buyerSeatIdOnDeal   — the seat ID the publisher configured on the deal at the SSP
  dspRegisteredSeatId — your DSP's registered seat ID

If these do not match EXACTLY (including case sensitivity), the SSP sends bid requests to a seat that does not exist in your DSP, so your DSP receives nothing.

Example mismatch:
  buyerSeatIdOnDeal:    seat-adtech-001
  dspRegisteredSeatId:  ADTECH-001
These are different strings — the SSP will send to seat-adtech-001 and your DSP ignores it.

Fix: Contact the publisher/SSP to update the buyerSeatId on the deal to exactly match your dspRegisteredSeatId. This requires a deal amendment.

CAUSE 2 — DEAL NOT ACTIVATED ON DSP
DealStatus may show ACTIVE on the SSP but PENDING or NOT_SYNCED on the DSP. The sync between SSP and DSP can take up to 2 hours after deal creation or modification.
Fix: Wait 2 hours after deal activation. If still not synced, trigger a manual re-sync from your DSP deal management UI.

CAUSE 3 — FLIGHT DATES
Deal flightStart must be today or earlier. Deal flightEnd must be in the future.
Check getDealStatus.flightStart and flightEnd fields.

CAUSE 4 — FIXED PRICE BELOW YOUR BID
For PG (Programmatic Guaranteed) deals, the deal has a fixedPrice. Your line item bid must match the fixedPrice exactly or the SSP drops the bid request.

CAUSE 5 — TARGETING CONFLICT
If your line item targets only US mobile traffic but the deal's eligible inventory is global desktop, the intersection is empty and no bid requests match both filters. Widen your line item targeting to match the deal's inventory scope.
